That is context-aware already, you know, and they all wish they had a Glean-like experience where they could search the web and search internally and then be able to store that so that their marketing can continue to use it, you know.
So that's kind of like a way to kind of think about it, you know, it's like there's so much here that we've already built.
It's just like, you know, being respectful of time, too, on your end, but it's like there's a lot here that we've already built that's going to continue to build leverage.
And then one
Also, that's cool to call out.
We're not the main constraint.
We're booked through the end of September already, and we only do two kickoffs a week intentionally just to make sure we're maintaining the quality.
And so for us, it's mostly like opening it up intentionally to three and then four.
So for us to get to $47 million next year, it's essentially four kickoffs a week on average towards starting in Q2.
Yeah, it sounds like you don't feel a lot of ACV pressure.
It's been going up.
It went from 140 to 180.
Yeah, interesting.
Yeah, so it's more for deploy folks and just landing more customers that you can kick off every week and get rolling.
Yeah, and we haven't invested in go-to-market, you know, a whole lot.
just hired first AE four weeks ago.
And so our goal is to hire a couple more AEs and then invest more aggressively in marketing.
It's just...
just...
The man hasn't been the limiting factor here.
Yeah.
It's mostly engineering horsepower to throw.
I mean, these are massive endeavors, right?
Like these are not like, it's a tiny box in this slide, but I mean, the amount we build and the amount we need to continue to build is pretty, pretty large footprint.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And I guess, how much are you looking to raise in the B?
Are you burning capital right now?
Like, yeah, so looking to raise 40 to 60 opportunistically, we burn only 237 in August.
We're still finished closing the books and we added, I think one or 2 million in run rate in August.
And so highly capital efficient, you know, most months while under 0.5 burn multiple and our magic number is well above 15 most months.
And so like, we're definitely like under invested as opposed to like, you know, cranking through outbound sales.
think is all you know,
In to get to growth, right?
It's more the opposite.
All our deals have been inbound and word of mouth.
Like we've never outbounded to anybody, you know?

And so for us, like fundraising was more of we're hitting this crazy additional inflection due to AI answers and like, and just like the timing and everything else is kind of like this perfect combination.
So then we started getting a ton of inbound from investors and we just thought like the timing might be opportunistic.
And so like, we've met with a bunch of people this week and, and then, you know, between us, like, I don't know if I have that many more weeks in me.
So it's like, well, we'll run a process probably, you know, until next week and then decide whether we want to raise or not.
And cause we got to get back to business, you know what I mean?
It's like, so I'm kind of like, we have a lot of angels also offering to write checks.
And so maybe we'll do a safe if we want to, but, but it's like, we have a lot of options but we're capital efficient.
We'll be profitable again in two, three months if we want to.
Yeah.
Yeah.
Oh, that's great.
It's a good spot to be.
And we only burned 3 million lifetime of the company to get to 11 million.
So that's mostly in the last like several months hiring ahead on the engineering side to build a platform.
Yeah, cool.
That's awesome.
That's great.
It sounds like you're in a good spot.
Well, thanks for this.
This was really helpful to hear it from you.
And again, thanks for sharing the deck.
Let Monica and I circle back on our side.
And again, like part of this is a little bit of calibration too for me just being, again, my third kid at M12.
So as I'm gathering context, I'm looking at a lot of things and building pieces, areas, and that sort of thing.
But again, like my wife's a marketer and, you know, I've lived through her both at small companies and now she's at Twelio.
And so I've seen, I've seen this world firsthand.
And.
And.
So it's totally, the story totally resonates with me.
And I've seen the agency clunkiness through my time at Twitter too.
It's like, it's amazing what kind of some of these big brands need help doing and the dollars that are sloshed around there versus, again, tech budgets or tooling budget.
That totally resonates with me.
Yeah, it's like a crazy fight to earn a dollar of infra or software.
And then at agencies, it's like, you're paying an how much?
Yeah, yeah, exactly.
So I can have lunch with you and tell you.
Just to give you a fun example, I'll let you go.
But when I was at HPE, I was brought in and there's a corporate marketing.
And I was like, who are we working with?
Oh, this agency called Razorfish.
I go in and I say, hey, how much are we paying them?
And I was like, so I hit up the account manager on it.
I was like, can you please send me like a timesheet or something of like how the.
Yeah, what are you guys doing?
She sent me, first of all, she's completely caught off guard.
Clearly, she didn't have it.
So a week later, she sends me this spreadsheet for the first time.
And there was 12 SVPs listed there that I had never met in my life.
And they were about $750 to $2,500 an hour each.
So this  is happening every single day inside of these agencies.
And they're getting away with murder for doing nothing.
It's just large companies don't have the scrutiny, so they don't care.
And they're just throwing money at it.
Small companies don't have an option, so they go for smaller ones.
There are capacity constraints.
And so I just lived it for so long that I'm like, this is insane.
People are literally insane here.
We should solve it.
Yeah, cool.
It's awesome.
Well, yeah, thanks for the time.
This is awesome.
This is great to connect.
And thanks for the materials.
We'll review it.
I'll link up with Monica and give you a point of view of where we are.
Perfect.
Sounds good.
Thanks.
Appreciate it.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Introduce GrowthX to M12 and discuss the company's AI-powered marketing services platform and potential investment opportunity.](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX offers a high-margin (70%+) AI-enabled marketing service, targeting the $500B agency/freelancer market](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=214.0)
  - [Platform combines AI workflows, expert marketplace, and last-mile execution to deliver comprehensive marketing solutions](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=624.0)
  - [Strong traction with 40% win rate, $180K ACV, and customers like Ramp, Webflow, and Sentinel One](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=422.0)
  - [Considering $40-60M Series B raise opportunistically, but capital-efficient with low burn ($237K in August)](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=2067.0)

## Topics

### GrowthX's Market Opportunity and Positioning

  - [Targeting $500B marketing services market (agencies/freelancers), not software](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=214.0)
  - [Positioned as a service, not a tool, enabling faster sales cycles and higher ACVs](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=522.0)
  - [No direct competition in bake-offs; differentiated by combining top experts, AI platform, and credibility](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1647.0)

### Product and Technology Overview

  - [Internal platform powers service delivery, with plans to expose as customer dashboard](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=864.0)
  - [AI workflow framework includes context engine, knowledge base, and execution layer](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1289.0)
  - [Agentic workflows built as code for scalability and continuous improvement](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1289.0)
  - [Runtime layer processed 3M+ workflow runs in recent months](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1196.0)

### Team and Operational Structure

  - [70+ team members: \~30 in build team (engineers, ops, GTM) and \~40 forward-deploy experts](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1570.0)
  - [Forward-deploy experts include directors, strategists, managing editors, and content managers](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1589.0)

### Customer Success and Results

  - [Example: Augment Code - 80 high-quality content pieces in 14 weeks, 10x increase in AI visibility](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1216.0)
  - [Ramp - 700+ pages published, including entire vendor site section](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1260.0)

### Business Model and Financials

  - [70%+ gross margins, trending towards 80%](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=555.0)
  - [$11M ARR reached with only $3M total burn](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1163.0)
  - [Projecting $47M ARR next year with increased kickoff capacity](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=2006.0)

### Future Plans and Potential Fundraise

  - [Expanding expert marketplace for increased leverage](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1354.0)
  - [Developing more user-friendly workflow IDE for non-engineers](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1888.0)
  - [Considering $40-60M Series B raise, but capital-efficient with options to remain profitable](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=2067.0)

## Next Steps

  - M12 team (Peter and Monica) to review materials and discuss internally
  - M12 to provide a point of view on potential investment
  - [GrowthX to decide on fundraising process timeline, potentially concluding next week](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=2124.0)
  - [GrowthX to continue scaling operations, aiming for 3-4 customer kickoffs per week by Q2 next year](https://fathom.video/share/BRmJHW4FaAiVcVrxc_uztMBnQAARF3sb?tab=summary&timestamp=1992.0)

action_items.description: Review GrowthX materials w/ Monica. Discuss internally. Formulate M12's stance.
Circle back w/ Marcel post-internal discussion. Provide M12's point of view on GrowthX opportunity.
